concerns.text: Task T011 applies MNE-Python preprocessing (ICA, band-pass) to *simulated* signals. This violates FR-002 which mandates preprocessing 'resting-state EEG recordings' (real data) and Constitution Principle VI which requires raw EEG from TUH/HCP to be stored and processed. The task silently relaxes the requirement to process real biological data to processing synthetic data, invalidating the 'Data Hygiene' and 'Neuroimaging Data Integrity' constraints.

Task T012 implements ICA-based artifact rejection (>30% channels removed) on *simulated* data. This violates SC-004 (measuring quality of 'dMRI and EEG preprocessing pipelines' on real data) and Constitution Principle VI. Simulated data does not contain ocular/muscle artifacts; applying ICA rejection logic here is a semantic weakening of the data quality requirement, turning a real-world hygiene check into a meaningless simulation step.

Task T009 fetches dMRI from OpenNeuro ds003813. FR-001 explicitly requires downloading from 'HCP' using 'HCP multimodal parcellation'. While the Plan justifies a data source switch due to availability, the Task does not explicitly state this deviation as a 'staged simplification' or 'simulation approach' in its description, creating a silent drift from the FR-001 constraint without the necessary constitutional justification in the task text itself.

Task T015 thresholds at the 75th percentile. FR-004 requires thresholding at the 75th percentile 'calculated per-participant over the entire resting-state recording'. Since the Plan switches to simulation, the task must explicitly clarify if the 'entire recording' refers to the simulated time-series length or if the threshold logic is adapted. Without this, the task risks applying a real-data statistical definition to synthetic data without semantic preservation of the 'resting-state' context.
